  6‚Üí *  Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later
  7‚Üí *  version.
  8‚Üí *
  9‚Üí *  This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY
 10‚Üí *  WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
 11‚Üí *  PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12‚Üí *
 13‚Üí *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14‚Üí *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15‚Üí */
 16‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer
 17‚Üí
 18‚Üíimport android.content.Context
 19‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 20‚Üíimport android.view.KeyEvent
 21‚Üíimport androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 22‚Üíimport androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatDelegate
 23‚Üíimport androidx.core.content.edit
 24‚Üíimport androidx.core.os.LocaleListCompat
 25‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.Gesture
 26‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 27‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.noteeditor.CustomToolbarButton
 28‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 29‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding
 30‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
 31‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 32‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.FullScreenMode
 33‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 34‚Üíimport com.ichi2.libanki.Consts
 35‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.HashSetInit
 36‚Üíimport timber.log.Timber
 37‚Üíimport java.util.*
 38‚Üíimport kotlin.collections.ArrayList
 39‚Üí
 40‚Üíprivate typealias VersionIdentifier = Int
 41‚Üíprivate typealias LegacyVersionIdentifier = Long
 42‚Üí
 43‚Üíobject PreferenceUpgradeService {
 44‚Üí    fun upgradePreferences(context: Context, previousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier): Boolean =
 45‚Üí        upgradePreferences(context.sharedPrefs(), previousVersionCode)
 46‚Üí
 47‚Üí    /** @return Whether any preferences were upgraded */
 48‚Üí    internal fun upgradePreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, previousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier): Boolean {
 49‚Üí        val pendingPreferenceUpgrades = PreferenceUpgrade.getPendingUpgrades(preferences, previousVersionCode)
 50‚Üí
 51‚Üí        pendingPreferenceUpgrades.forEach {
 52‚Üí            it.performUpgrade(preferences)
 53‚Üí        }
 54‚Üí
 55‚Üí        return pendingPreferenceUpgrades.isNotEmpty()
 56‚Üí    }
 57‚Üí
 58‚Üí    /**
 59‚Üí     * Specifies that no preference upgrades need to happen.
 60‚Üí     * Typically because the app has been run for the first time, or the preferences
 61‚Üí     * have been deleted
 62‚Üí     */
 63‚Üí    @JvmStatic // reqired for mockito for now
 64‚Üí    fun setPreferencesUpToDate(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 65‚Üí        Timber.i("Marking preferences as up to date")
 66‚Üí        PreferenceUpgrade.setPreferenceToLatestVersion(preferences)
 67‚Üí    }
 68‚Üí
 69‚Üí    abstract class PreferenceUpgrade private constructor(val versionIdentifier: VersionIdentifier) {
 70‚Üí        /*
 71‚Üí        To add a new preference upgrade:
 72‚Üí          * yield a new class from getAllInstances (do not use `legacyPreviousVersionCode` in the constructor)
 73‚Üí          * Implement the upgrade() method
 74‚Üí          * Set mVersionIdentifier to 1 more than the previous versionIdentifier
 75‚Üí          * Run tests in PreferenceUpgradeServiceTest
 76‚Üí         */
 77‚Üí
 78‚Üí        companion object {
 79‚Üí            /** A version code where the value doesn't matter as we're not using the result */
 80‚Üí            private const val IGNORED_LEGACY_VERSION_CODE = 0L
 81‚Üí            const val upgradeVersionPrefKey = "preferenceUpgradeVersion"
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí            /** Returns all instances of preference upgrade classes */
 84‚Üí            internal fun getAllInstances(legacyPreviousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier) = sequence<PreferenceUpgrade> {
 85‚Üí                yield(LegacyPreferenceUpgrade(legacyPreviousVersionCode))
 86‚Üí                yield(UpdateNoteEditorToolbarPrefs())
 87‚Üí                yield(UpgradeGesturesToControls())
 88‚Üí                yield(UpgradeDayAndNightThemes())
 89‚Üí                yield(UpgradeFetchMedia())
 90‚Üí                yield(UpgradeAppLocale())
 91‚Üí            }
 92‚Üí
 93‚Üí            /** Returns a list of preference upgrade classes which have not been applied */
 94‚Üí            fun getPendingUpgrades(preferences: SharedPreferences, legacyPreviousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier): List<PreferenceUpgrade> {
 95‚Üí                val currentPrefVersion: VersionIdentifier = getPreferenceVersion(preferences)
 96‚Üí
 97‚Üí                return getAllInstances(legacyPreviousVersionCode).filter {
 98‚Üí                    it.versionIdentifier > currentPrefVersion
 99‚Üí                }.toList()
100‚Üí            }
101‚Üí
102‚Üí            /** Sets the preference version such that no upgrades need to be applied */
103‚Üí            fun setPreferenceToLatestVersion(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
104‚Üí                val versionWhichRequiresNoUpgrades = getLatestVersion()
105‚Üí                setPreferenceVersion(preferences, versionWhichRequiresNoUpgrades)
106‚Üí            }
107‚Üí
108‚Üí            internal fun getPreferenceVersion(preferences: SharedPreferences) =
109‚Üí                preferences.getInt(upgradeVersionPrefKey, 0)
110‚Üí
111‚Üí            internal fun setPreferenceVersion(preferences: SharedPreferences, versionIdentifier: VersionIdentifier) {
112‚Üí                Timber.i("upgrading preference version to '$versionIdentifier'")
113‚Üí                preferences.edit { putInt(upgradeVersionPrefKey, versionIdentifier) }
114‚Üí            }
115‚Üí
116‚Üí            /** Returns the collection of all preference version numbers */
117‚Üí            @VisibleForTesting
118‚Üí            fun getAllVersionIdentifiers(): Sequence<VersionIdentifier> =
119‚Üí                getAllInstances(IGNORED_LEGACY_VERSION_CODE).map { it.versionIdentifier }
120‚Üí
121‚Üí            /**
122‚Üí             * @return the latest "version" of the preferences
123‚Üí             * If the preferences are set to this version, then no upgrades will take place
124‚Üí             */
125‚Üí            private fun getLatestVersion(): VersionIdentifier = getAllVersionIdentifiers().maxOrNull() ?: 0
126‚Üí        }
127‚Üí
128‚Üí        /** Handles preference upgrades before 2021-08-01,
129‚Üí         * upgrades were detected via a version code comparison
130‚Üí         * rather than comparing a preference value
131‚Üí         */
132‚Üí        private class LegacyPreferenceUpgrade(val previousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier) : PreferenceUpgrade(1) {
133‚Üí            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
134‚Üí                if (!needsLegacyPreferenceUpgrade(previousVersionCode)) {
135‚Üí                    return
136‚Üí                }
137‚Üí
138‚Üí                Timber.i("running upgradePreferences()")
139‚Üí                // clear all prefs if super old version to prevent any errors
140‚Üí                if (previousVersionCode < 20300130) {
141‚Üí                    Timber.i("Old version of Anki - Clearing preferences")
142‚Üí                    preferences.edit { clear() }
143‚Üí                }
144‚Üí                // when upgrading from before 2.5alpha35
145‚Üí                if (previousVersionCode < 20500135) {
146‚Üí                    Timber.i("Old version of Anki - Fixing Zoom")
147‚Üí                    // Card zooming behaviour was changed the preferences renamed
148‚Üí                    val oldCardZoom = preferences.getInt("relativeDisplayFontSize", 100)
149‚Üí                    val oldImageZoom = preferences.getInt("relativeImageSize", 100)
150‚Üí                    preferences.edit {
151‚Üí                        putInt("cardZoom", oldCardZoom)
152‚Üí                        putInt("imageZoom", oldImageZoom)
153‚Üí                    }
154‚Üí                    if (!preferences.getBoolean("useBackup", true)) {
155‚Üí                        preferences.edit { putInt("backupMax", 0) }
156‚Üí                    }
157‚Üí                    preferences.edit {
158‚Üí                        remove("useBackup")
159‚Üí                        remove("intentAdditionInstantAdd")
160‚Üí                    }
161‚Üí                }
162‚Üí                FullScreenMode.upgradeFromLegacyPreference(preferences)
163‚Üí            }
164‚Üí
165‚Üí            fun needsLegacyPreferenceUpgrade(previous: Long): Boolean = previous < CHECK_PREFERENCES_AT_VERSION
166‚Üí
167‚Üí            companion object {
168‚Üí                /**
169‚Üí                 * The latest package version number that included changes to the preferences that requires handling. All
170‚Üí                 * collections being upgraded to (or after) this version must update preferences.
171‚Üí                 *
172‚Üí                 * #9309 Do not modify this variable - it no longer works.
173‚Üí                 *
174‚Üí                 * Instead, add an unconditional check for the old preference before the call to
175‚Üí                 * "needsPreferenceUpgrade", and perform the upgrade.
176‚Üí                 */
177‚Üí                const val CHECK_PREFERENCES_AT_VERSION = 20500225
178‚Üí            }
179‚Üí        }
180‚Üí
181‚Üí        fun performUpgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
182‚Üí            Timber.i("Running preference upgrade: ${this.javaClass.simpleName}")
183‚Üí            upgrade(preferences)
184‚Üí
185‚Üí            setPreferenceVersion(preferences, this.versionIdentifier)
186‚Üí        }
187‚Üí
188‚Üí        protected abstract fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences)
189‚Üí
190‚Üí        /**
191‚Üí         * update toolbar buttons with new preferences, when button text empty or null then it adds the index as button text
192‚Üí         */
193‚Üí        internal class UpdateNoteEditorToolbarPrefs : PreferenceUpgrade(4) {
194‚Üí            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
195‚Üí                val buttons = getNewToolbarButtons(preferences)
196‚Üí
197‚Üí                // update prefs
198‚Üí                preferences.edit {
199‚Üí                    remove("note_editor_custom_buttons")
200‚Üí                    putStringSet("note_editor_custom_buttons", CustomToolbarButton.toStringSet(buttons))
201‚Üí                }
202‚Üí            }
203‚Üí
204‚Üí            private fun getNewToolbarButtons(preferences: SharedPreferences): ArrayList<CustomToolbarButton> {
205‚Üí                // get old toolbar prefs
206‚Üí                val set = preferences.getStringSet("note_editor_custom_buttons", HashSetInit<String>(0)) as Set<String?>
207‚Üí                // new list with buttons size
208‚Üí                val buttons = ArrayList<CustomToolbarButton>(set.size)
209‚Üí
210‚Üí                // parse fields with separator
211‚Üí                for (s in set) {
212‚Üí                    val fields = s!!.split(Consts.FIELD_SEPARATOR.toRegex(), CustomToolbarButton.KEEP_EMPTY_ENTRIES.coerceAtLeast(0)).toTypedArray()
213‚Üí                    if (fields.size != 3) {
214‚Üí                        continue
215‚Üí                    }
216‚Üí
217‚Üí                    val index: Int = try {
218‚Üí                        fields[0].toInt()
219‚Üí                    } catch (e: Exception) {
220‚Üí                        Timber.w(e)
221‚Üí                        continue
222‚Üí                    }
223‚Üí
224‚Üí                    // add new button with the index + 1 as button text
225‚Üí                    val visualIndex: Int = index + 1
226‚Üí                    val buttonText = visualIndex.toString()
227‚Üí
228‚Üí                    // fields 1 is prefix, fields 2 is suffix
229‚Üí                    buttons.add(CustomToolbarButton(index, buttonText, fields[1], fields[2]))
230‚Üí                }
231‚Üí                return buttons
232‚Üí            }
233‚Üí        }
234‚Üí
235‚Üí        internal class UpgradeGesturesToControls : PreferenceUpgrade(5) {
236‚Üí            val oldCommandValues = mapOf(
237‚Üí                Pair(1, ViewerCommand.SHOW_ANSWER),
238‚Üí                Pair(2, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1),
239‚Üí                Pair(3, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2),
240‚Üí                Pair(4, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3),
241‚Üí                Pair(5, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4),
242‚Üí                Pair(6, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_RECOMMENDED),
243‚Üí                Pair(7, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_BETTER_THAN_RECOMMENDED),
244‚Üí                Pair(8, ViewerCommand.UNDO),
245‚Üí                Pair(9, ViewerCommand.EDIT),
246‚Üí                Pair(10, ViewerCommand.MARK),
247‚Üí                Pair(12, ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD),
248‚Üí                Pair(13, ViewerCommand.SUSPEND_CARD),
249‚Üí                Pair(14, ViewerCommand.DELETE),
250‚Üí                Pair(16, ViewerCommand.PLAY_MEDIA),
251‚Üí                Pair(17, ViewerCommand.EXIT),
252‚Üí                Pair(18, ViewerCommand.BURY_NOTE),
253‚Üí                Pair(19, ViewerCommand.SUSPEND_NOTE),
254‚Üí                Pair(20, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_RED),
255‚Üí                Pair(21, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE),
256‚Üí                Pair(22, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN),
257‚Üí                Pair(23, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE),
258‚Üí                Pair(38, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK),
259‚Üí                Pair(39, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE),
260‚Üí                Pair(40, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE),
261‚Üí                Pair(24, ViewerCommand.UNSET_FLAG),
262‚Üí                Pair(30, ViewerCommand.PAGE_UP),
263‚Üí                Pair(31, ViewerCommand.PAGE_DOWN),
264‚Üí                Pair(32, ViewerCommand.TAG),
265‚Üí                Pair(33, ViewerCommand.CARD_INFO),
266‚Üí                Pair(34, ViewerCommand.ABORT_AND_SYNC),
267‚Üí                Pair(35, ViewerCommand.RECORD_VOICE),
268‚Üí                Pair(36, ViewerCommand.REPLAY_VOICE),
269‚Üí                Pair(37, ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD),
270‚Üí                Pair(44, ViewerCommand.CLEAR_WHITEBOARD),
271‚Üí                Pair(45, ViewerCommand.CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR),
272‚Üí                Pair(41, ViewerCommand.SHOW_HINT),
273‚Üí                Pair(42, ViewerCommand.SHOW_ALL_HINTS),
274‚Üí                Pair(43, ViewerCommand.ADD_NOTE)
275‚Üí            )
276‚Üí
277‚Üí            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
278‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureSwipeUp", Gesture.SWIPE_UP)
279‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureSwipeDown", Gesture.SWIPE_DOWN)
280‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureSwipeLeft", Gesture.SWIPE_LEFT)
281‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureSwipeRight", Gesture.SWIPE_RIGHT)
282‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureLongclick", Gesture.LONG_TAP)
283‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureDoubleTap", Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP)
284‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapTopLeft", Gesture.TAP_TOP_LEFT)
285‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapTop", Gesture.TAP_TOP)
286‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapTopRight", Gesture.TAP_TOP_RIGHT)
287‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapLeft", Gesture.TAP_LEFT)
288‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapCenter", Gesture.TAP_CENTER)
289‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapRight", Gesture.TAP_RIGHT)
290‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapBottomLeft", Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_LEFT)
291‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapBottom", Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM)
292‚Üí                upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureTapBottomRight", Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_RIGHT)
293‚Üí                upgradeVolumeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureVolumeUp", KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP)
294‚Üí                upgradeVolumeGestureToBinding(preferences, "gestureVolumeDown", KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN)
295‚Üí            }
296‚Üí
297‚Üí            private fun upgradeVolumeGestureToBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, volumeKeyCode: Int) {
298‚Üí                upgradeBinding(preferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey, keyCode(volumeKeyCode))
299‚Üí            }
300‚Üí
301‚Üí            private fun upgradeGestureToBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, gesture: Gesture) {
302‚Üí                upgradeBinding(preferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey, Binding.gesture(gesture))
303‚Üí            }
304‚Üí
305‚Üí            @VisibleForTesting
306‚Üí            internal fun upgradeBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, binding: Binding) {
307‚Üí                Timber.d("Replacing gesture '%s' with binding", oldGesturePreferenceKey)
308‚Üí
309‚Üí                // This exists as a user may have mapped "volume down" to "UNDO".
310‚Üí                // Undo already exists as a key binding, and we don't want to trash this during an upgrade
311‚Üí                if (!preferences.contains(oldGesturePreferenceKey)) {
312‚Üí                    Timber.v("No preference to upgrade")
313‚Üí                    return
314‚Üí                }
315‚Üí
316‚Üí                try {
317‚Üí                    replaceBinding(preferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey, binding)
318‚Üí                } finally {
319‚Üí                    Timber.v("removing pref key: '%s'", oldGesturePreferenceKey)
320‚Üí                    // remove the old key
321‚Üí                    preferences.edit { remove(oldGesturePreferenceKey) }
322‚Üí                }
323‚Üí            }
324‚Üí
325‚Üí            private fun replaceBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, binding: Binding) {
326‚Üí                // the preference should be set, but if it's null, then we have nothing to do
327‚Üí                val pref = preferences.getString(oldGesturePreferenceKey, "0") ?: return
328‚Üí                // If the preference doesn't map (for example: it was removed), then nothing to do
329‚Üí                val asInt = pref.toIntOrNull() ?: return
330‚Üí                val command = oldCommandValues[asInt] ?: return
331‚Üí
332‚Üí                Timber.i("Moving preference from '%s' to '%s'", oldGesturePreferenceKey, command.preferenceKey)
333‚Üí
334‚Üí                // add to the binding_COMMANDNAME preference
335‚Üí                val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(CardSide.BOTH))
336‚Üí                command.addBindingAtEnd(preferences, mappableBinding)
337‚Üí            }
338‚Üí        }
339‚Üí        internal class UpgradeDayAndNightThemes : PreferenceUpgrade(6) {
340‚Üí            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
341‚Üí                val dayTheme = preferences.getString("dayTheme", "0")
342‚Üí                val nightTheme = preferences.getString("nightTheme", "0")
343‚Üí
344‚Üí                preferences.edit {
345‚Üí                    if (dayTheme == "1") { // plain
346‚Üí                        putString("dayTheme", "2")
347‚Üí                    } else { // light
348‚Üí                        putString("dayTheme", "1")
349‚Üí                    }
350‚Üí                    if (nightTheme == "1") { // dark
351‚Üí                        putString("nightTheme", "4")
352‚Üí                    } else { // black
353‚Üí                        putString("nightTheme", "3")
354‚Üí                    }
355‚Üí                    remove("invertedColors")
356‚Üí                }
357‚Üí            }
358‚Üí        }
359‚Üí
360‚Üí        internal class UpgradeFetchMedia : PreferenceUpgrade(9) {
361‚Üí            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
362‚Üí                val fetchMediaSwitch = preferences.getBoolean(RemovedPreferences.SYNC_FETCHES_MEDIA, true)
363‚Üí                val status = if (fetchMediaSwitch) "always" else "never"
364‚Üí                preferences.edit {
365‚Üí                    remove(RemovedPreferences.SYNC_FETCHES_MEDIA)
366‚Üí                    putString("syncFetchMedia", status)
367‚Üí                }
368‚Üí            }
369‚Üí        }
370‚Üí
371‚Üí        internal class UpgradeAppLocale : PreferenceUpgrade(10) {
372‚Üí            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
373‚Üí                fun getLocale(localeCode: String): Locale {
374‚Üí                    // Language separators are '_' or '-' at different times in display/resource fetch
375‚Üí                    val locale: Locale = if (localeCode.contains("_") || localeCode.contains("-")) {
376‚Üí                        try {
377‚Üí                            val localeParts = localeCode.split("[_-]".toRegex(), 2).toTypedArray()
378‚Üí                            Locale(localeParts[0], localeParts[1])
379‚Üí                        } catch (e: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException) {
380‚Üí                            Timber.w(e, "getLocale variant split fail, using code '%s' raw.", localeCode)
381‚Üí                            Locale(localeCode)
382‚Üí                        }
383‚Üí                    } else {
384‚Üí                        Locale(localeCode) // guaranteed to be non null
385‚Üí                    }
386‚Üí                    return locale
387‚Üí                }
388‚Üí                // 1. upgrade value from `locale.toString()` to `locale.toLanguageTag()`,
389‚Üí                // because the new API uses language tags
390‚Üí                val languagePrefValue = preferences.getString("language", "")!!
391‚Üí                val languageTag = if (languagePrefValue.isNotEmpty()) {
392‚Üí                    getLocale(languagePrefValue).toLanguageTag()
393‚Üí                } else {
394‚Üí                    null
395‚Üí                }
396‚Üí                preferences.edit {
397‚Üí                    putString("language", languageTag ?: "")
398‚Üí                }
399‚Üí                // 2. Set the locale with the new AndroidX API
400‚Üí                val localeList = LocaleListCompat.forLanguageTags(languageTag)

 16‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 17‚Üí
 18‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 19‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.GestureMapper
 20‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 21‚Üí
 22‚Üíclass GestureProcessor(private val processor: ViewerCommand.CommandProcessor?) {
 23‚Üí    companion object {
 24‚Üí        const val PREF_KEY = "gestures"
 25‚Üí    }
 26‚Üí    private var gestureDoubleTap: ViewerCommand? = null
 27‚Üí    private var gestureLongclick: ViewerCommand? = null
 28‚Üí    private var gestureSwipeUp: ViewerCommand? = null
 29‚Üí    private var gestureSwipeDown: ViewerCommand? = null
 30‚Üí    private var gestureSwipeLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 31‚Üí    private var gestureSwipeRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 32‚Üí    private var gestureTapLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 33‚Üí    private var gestureTapRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 34‚Üí    private var gestureTapTop: ViewerCommand? = null
 35‚Üí    private var gestureTapBottom: ViewerCommand? = null
 36‚Üí    private var gestureTapTopLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 37‚Üí    private var gestureTapTopRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 38‚Üí    private var gestureTapCenter: ViewerCommand? = null
 39‚Üí    private var gestureTapBottomLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 40‚Üí    private var gestureTapBottomRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 41‚Üí    private val gestureMapper = GestureMapper()
 42‚Üí
 43‚Üí    /**
 44‚Üí     * Whether the class has been enabled.
 45‚Üí     * This requires the "gestures" preference is enabled,
 46‚Üí     * and [GestureProcessor.init] has been called
 47‚Üí     */
 48‚Üí    var isEnabled = false
 49‚Üí        private set
 50‚Üí
 51‚Üí    fun init(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 52‚Üí        isEnabled = preferences.getBoolean(PREF_KEY, false)
 53‚Üí
 54‚Üí        val associatedCommands = HashMap<Gesture, ViewerCommand>()
 55‚Üí        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
 56‚Üí            for (mappableBinding in MappableBinding.fromPreference(preferences, command)) {
 57‚Üí                if (mappableBinding.binding.isGesture) {
 58‚Üí                    associatedCommands[mappableBinding.binding.gesture!!] = command
 59‚Üí                }
 60‚Üí            }
 61‚Üí        }
 62‚Üí        gestureDoubleTap = associatedCommands[Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP]
 63‚Üí        gestureLongclick = associatedCommands[Gesture.LONG_TAP]
 64‚Üí        gestureSwipeUp = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_UP]
 65‚Üí        gestureSwipeDown = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_DOWN]
 66‚Üí        gestureSwipeLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_LEFT]
 67‚Üí        gestureSwipeRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_RIGHT]
 68‚Üí        gestureMapper.init(preferences)
 69‚Üí        gestureTapLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_LEFT]
 70‚Üí        gestureTapRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_RIGHT]
 71‚Üí        gestureTapTop = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_TOP]
 72‚Üí        gestureTapBottom = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM]
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üí        val useCornerTouch = preferences.getBoolean("gestureCornerTouch", false)
 75‚Üí        if (useCornerTouch) {
 76‚Üí            gestureTapTopLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_TOP_LEFT]
 77‚Üí            gestureTapTopRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_TOP_RIGHT]
 78‚Üí            gestureTapCenter = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_CENTER]
 79‚Üí            gestureTapBottomLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_LEFT]
 80‚Üí            gestureTapBottomRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_RIGHT]
 81‚Üí        }
 82‚Üí    }
 83‚Üí
 84‚Üí    fun onTap(height: Int, width: Int, posX: Float, posY: Float): Boolean? {
 85‚Üí        val gesture = gestureMapper.gesture(height, width, posX, posY) ?: return false
 86‚Üí        return execute(gesture)
 87‚Üí    }
 88‚Üí
 89‚Üí    fun onDoubleTap(): Boolean? {
 90‚Üí        return execute(Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP)
 91‚Üí    }
 92‚Üí
 93‚Üí    fun onLongTap(): Boolean? {
 94‚Üí        return execute(Gesture.LONG_TAP)
 95‚Üí    }
 96‚Üí
 97‚Üí    fun onFling(dx: Float, dy: Float, velocityX: Float, velocityY: Float, isSelecting: Boolean, isXScrolling: Boolean, isYScrolling: Boolean): Boolean? {
 98‚Üí        val gesture = gestureMapper.gesture(dx, dy, velocityX, velocityY, isSelecting, isXScrolling, isYScrolling)
 99‚Üí        return execute(gesture)
100‚Üí    }
101‚Üí
102‚Üí    private fun execute(gesture: Gesture?): Boolean? {
103‚Üí        val command = mapGestureToCommand(gesture)
104‚Üí        return if (command != null) {
105‚Üí            processor?.executeCommand(command, gesture)
106‚Üí        } else {
107‚Üí            false
108‚Üí        }
109‚Üí    }
110‚Üí
111‚Üí    private fun mapGestureToCommand(gesture: Gesture?): ViewerCommand? {
112‚Üí        return when (gesture) {
113‚Üí            Gesture.SWIPE_UP -> gestureSwipeUp
114‚Üí            Gesture.SWIPE_DOWN -> gestureSwipeDown
115‚Üí            Gesture.SWIPE_LEFT -> gestureSwipeLeft
116‚Üí            Gesture.SWIPE_RIGHT -> gestureSwipeRight
117‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_TOP -> gestureTapTop
118‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_TOP_LEFT -> gestureTapTopLeft
119‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_TOP_RIGHT -> gestureTapTopRight
120‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_LEFT -> gestureTapLeft
121‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_CENTER -> gestureTapCenter
122‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_RIGHT -> gestureTapRight
123‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM -> gestureTapBottom
124‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_LEFT -> gestureTapBottomLeft
125‚Üí            Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_RIGHT -> gestureTapBottomRight
126‚Üí            Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP -> gestureDoubleTap
127‚Üí            Gesture.LONG_TAP -> gestureLongclick
128‚Üí            else -> null
129‚Üí        }
130‚Üí    }
131‚Üí
132‚Üí    /**
133‚Üí     * Whether one of the provided gestures is bound
134‚Üí     * @param gestures the gestures to check
135‚Üí     * @return `false` if none of the gestures are bound. `true` otherwise
136‚Üí     */
137‚Üí    fun isBound(vararg gestures: Gesture): Boolean {
138‚Üí        if (!isEnabled) {
139‚Üí            return false
140‚Üí        }
141‚Üí        for (gesture in gestures) {
142‚Üí            if (mapGestureToCommand(gesture) != null) {
143‚Üí                return true
144‚Üí            }
145‚Üí        }
146‚Üí        return false
147‚Üí    }
148‚Üí}
149‚Üí

package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer

import android.content.SharedPreferences
import android.view.KeyEvent
import androidx.core.content.edit
import com.ichi2.anki.R
import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.unicode

import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
import java.util.*
import java.util.function.BiFunction
import java.util.stream.Collectors

/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.gesture_answer_1),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.gesture_answer_2),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.gesture_answer_3),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.gesture_answer_4),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_RECOMMENDED(R.string.gesture_answer_green),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_BETTER_THAN_RECOMMENDED(R.string.gesture_answer_better_recommended),
    UNDO(R.string.undo),
    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note);

    companion object {
        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
                .collect(Collectors.toList())
    }

    val preferenceKey: String
        get() = "binding_$name"

    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
            collection.remove(element)
            collection.add(0, element)
            true
        }
        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
    }

    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
            // do not reorder the elements
            if (collection.contains(element)) {
                return@BiFunction false
            }
            collection.add(element)
            return@BiFunction true
        }
        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
    }

    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
    }

    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
        bindings.remove(binding)
        prefs.edit {
            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
        }
    }

    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
            when (this) {
                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
                )
                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
                )
                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER)
                )
                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
                )
                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_RECOMMENDED -> from(
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
                )
                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
                else -> ArrayList()
            }

    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
    }

    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
    }

    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
    }

    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
    }

    fun interface CommandProcessor {
        /**
         *
         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
         */
        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
    }
}

content.thinking:
**Preparing final summary of changes**

I‚Äôm organizing a clear summary of the updates I made, including adding the new reschedule command with preference and analytics support, wiring it into the reviewer to open the dialog on gestures, and updating tests to cover the new command. I‚Äôll also note that tests were not run since the user didn‚Äôt request it. This wraps everything up nicely!

content.text:
Summary:
- Added a new `ViewerCommand.RESCHEDULE_CARD`, giving it the existing ‚ÄúReschedule‚Äù string resource so it automatically appears in the controller/gesture mapping UI and preference storage.
- Wired the reviewer‚Äôs command handler to invoke `showRescheduleCardDialog()` when the new command is triggered, enabling one-touch rescheduling via mapped buttons or gestures.
- Ensured analytics and preference tracking cover the new binding key and updated the `ViewerCommandTest` safeguard to include it.

Tests: Not run (not requested).

 81‚Üí    }
 82‚Üí
 83‚Üí    val preferenceKey: String
 84‚Üí        get() = "binding_$name"
 85‚Üí
 86‚Üí    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 87‚Üí        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 88‚Üí            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 89‚Üí            collection.remove(element)
 90‚Üí            collection.add(0, element)
 91‚Üí            true
 92‚Üí        }
 93‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 97‚Üí        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 98‚Üí            // do not reorder the elements
 99‚Üí            if (collection.contains(element)) {
100‚Üí                return@BiFunction false
101‚Üí            }
102‚Üí            collection.add(element)
103‚Üí            return@BiFunction true
104‚Üí        }
105‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
106‚Üí    }
107‚Üí
108‚Üí    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
109‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
110‚Üí        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
111‚Üí        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
112‚Üí        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
113‚Üí    }
114‚Üí
115‚Üí    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
116‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
117‚Üí        bindings.remove(binding)
118‚Üí        prefs.edit {
119‚Üí            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
120‚Üí        }
121‚Üí    }

205‚Üí        }

244‚Üí    protected open fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Int) {
245‚Üí        if (card == null) {
246‚Üí            return
247‚Üí        }
248‚Üí        launchCatchingTask {
249‚Üí            card.setUserFlag(flag)
250‚Üí            if (BackendFactory.defaultLegacySchema) {
251‚Üí                card.flush()
252‚Üí                /* Following code would allow to update value of {{cardFlag}}.
253‚Üí               Anki does not update this value when a flag is changed, so
254‚Üí               currently this code would do something that anki itself
255‚Üí               does not do. I hope in the future Anki will correct that
256‚Üí               and this code may becomes useful.
257‚Üí
258‚Üí            card._getQA(true); //force reload. Useful iff {{cardFlag}} occurs in the template
259‚Üí            if (sDisplayAnswer) {
260‚Üí                displayCardAnswer();
261‚Üí            } else {
262‚Üí                displayCardQuestion();
263‚Üí                } */
264‚Üí            } else {
265‚Üí                withCol {
266‚Üí                    newBackend.setUserFlagForCards(listOf(card.id), flag)
267‚Üí                }
268‚Üí            }
269‚Üí            refreshActionBar()
270‚Üí            onFlagChanged()
271‚Üí        }
272‚Üí    }

281‚Üí    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
282‚Üí        val extras = intent.extras
283‚Üí        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
284‚Üí            // deckId is not set, load default
285‚Üí            return
286‚Üí        }
287‚Üí        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
288‚Üí        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
289‚Üí
290‚Üí        // deckId does not exist, load default
291‚Üí        if (col.decks.get(did, _default = false) == null) {
292‚Üí            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
293‚Üí            return
294‚Üí        }
295‚Üí
296‚Üí        // Clear the undo history when selecting a new deck
297‚Üí        if (col.decks.selected() != did) {
298‚Üí            col.clearUndo()
299‚Üí        }
300‚Üí        // Select the deck
301‚Üí        col.decks.select(did)
302‚Üí        // Reset the schedule so that we get the counts for the currently selected deck
303‚Üí        col.sched.deferReset()
304‚Üí    }

318‚Üí    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
319‚Üí        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
320‚Üí        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
321‚Üí            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
322‚Üí            selectDeckFromExtra()
323‚Üí        }
324‚Üí        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
325‚Üí        // task to load a card, but since we send null
326‚Üí        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
327‚Üí        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
328‚Üí        if (prefWhiteboard) {
329‚Üí            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
330‚Üí            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
331‚Üí            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
332‚Üí            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
333‚Üí            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
334‚Üí            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
335‚Üí        }
336‚Üí        col.sched.deferReset() // Reset schedule in case card was previously loaded
337‚Üí        col.startTimebox()
338‚Üí        GetCard().runWithHandler(answerCardHandler(false))
339‚Üí        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
340‚Üí        // Add a weak reference to current activity so that scheduler can talk to to Activity
341‚Üí        sched!!.setContext(WeakReference(this))
342‚Üí
343‚Üí        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
344‚Üí        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {
345‚Üí            setFullScreen(this)
346‚Üí        }
347‚Üí        setRenderWorkaround(this)
348‚Üí    }

 43‚Üí
 44‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45‚Üí    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46‚Üí    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47‚Üí    private var sOptIn = false

 51‚Üí    /**
 52‚Üí     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53‚Üí     * Usage after that is static
 54‚Üí     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55‚Üí     *
 56‚Üí     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57‚Üí     */
 58‚Üí    @Synchronized
 59‚Üí    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60‚Üí        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61‚Üí        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62‚Üí            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63‚Üí            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64‚Üí                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65‚Üí                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66‚Üí                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67‚Üí            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68‚Üí                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69‚Üí                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70‚Üí                .withDefaultRequest(
 71‚Üí                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72‚Üí                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73‚Üí                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74‚Üí                        .applicationVersion(Integer.toString(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE))
 75‚Üí                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76‚Üí                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77‚Üí                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78‚Üí                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79‚Üí                )
 80‚Üí                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81‚Üí                .build()
 82‚Üí        }
 83‚Üí        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84‚Üí        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85‚Üí        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86‚Üí        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87‚Üí            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88‚Üí                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89‚Üí                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90‚Üí                optIn = newValue
 91‚Üí            }
 92‚Üí        }
 93‚Üí        return sAnalytics
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97‚Üí        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98‚Üí            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99‚Üí        }
100‚Üí        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104‚Üí        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105‚Üí            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106‚Üí        }
107‚Üí        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108‚Üí    }

112‚Üí        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"

117‚Üí    /**
118‚Üí     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119‚Üí     */
120‚Üí    @Synchronized
121‚Üí    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122‚Üí        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123‚Üí        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124‚Üí            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125‚Üí            if (thread == null) {
126‚Üí                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127‚Üí                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128‚Üí            }
129‚Üí            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130‚Üí        }
131‚Üí    }
132‚Üí
133‚Üí    /**
134‚Üí     * Reset the default exception handler
135‚Üí     */
136‚Üí    @Synchronized
137‚Üí    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138‚Üí        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139‚Üí        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140‚Üí    }

142‚Üí    /**
143‚Üí     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144‚Üí     */
145‚Üí    @set:Synchronized
146‚Üí    private var optIn: Boolean
147‚Üí        get() {
148‚Üí            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149‚Üí            return sOptIn

151‚Üí        private set(optIn) {
152‚Üí            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153‚Üí            sOptIn = optIn
154‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156‚Üí            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157‚Üí            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158‚Üí        }

170‚Üí    /**
171‚Üí     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172‚Üí     */
173‚Üí    @Synchronized
174‚Üí    fun reInitialize() {
175‚Üí        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176‚Üí        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178‚Üí        sAnalytics = null
179‚Üí        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180‚Üí        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181‚Üí    }

194‚Üí    /**
195‚Üí     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis

197‚Üí     *
198‚Üí     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199‚Üí     */
200‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202‚Üí        if (!optIn) {
203‚Üí            return
204‚Üí        }
205‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206‚Üí    }

208‚Üí    /**
209‚Üí     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210‚Üí     *
211‚Üí     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212‚Üí     * @param action   the action the user performed
213‚Üí     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214‚Üí     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215‚Üí     */
216‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218‚Üí        if (!optIn) {
219‚Üí            return
220‚Üí        }
221‚Üí        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222‚Üí        if (label != null) {
223‚Üí            event.eventLabel(label)
224‚Üí        }
225‚Üí        if (value != null) {
226‚Üí            event.eventValue(value)
227‚Üí        }
228‚Üí        event.sendAsync()
229‚Üí    }

231‚Üí    /**

233‚Üí     *

235‚Üí     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236‚Üí     */
237‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {

239‚Üí    }

241‚Üí    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242‚Üí    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243‚Üí        var cause: Throwable?
244‚Üí        var result = t
245‚Üí        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246‚Üí            result = cause!!
247‚Üí        }
248‚Üí        return result
249‚Üí    }

251‚Üí    /**
252‚Üí     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253‚Üí     *
254‚Üí     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255‚Üí     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256‚Üí     */
257‚Üí    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258‚Üí        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259‚Üí        if (!sOptIn) {
260‚Üí            return
261‚Üí        }
262‚Üí        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263‚Üí    }

265‚Üí    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266‚Üí        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267‚Üí        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268‚Üí
269‚Üí        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270‚Üí        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271‚Üí            true
272‚Üí        } else {
273‚Üí            hasSetDataDirectory()
274‚Üí        }
275‚Üí
276‚Üí        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277‚Üí    }

283‚Üí            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284‚Üí        }
285‚Üí        set(value) {
286‚Üí            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287‚Üí            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288‚Üí                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289‚Üí            }
290‚Üí        }

297‚Üí    /**
298‚Üí     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299‚Üí     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300‚Üí     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301‚Üí     */
302‚Üí    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303‚Üí        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304‚Üí            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305‚Üí            try {
306‚Üí                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307‚Üí                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308‚Üí            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309‚Üí                Timber.w(e)
310‚Üí                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311‚Üí            }
312‚Üí
313‚Üí            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314‚Üí            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315‚Üí            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316‚Üí            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317‚Üí            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318‚Üí            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319‚Üí
320‚Üí            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321‚Üí            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322‚Üí            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323‚Üí            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324‚Üí            try {
325‚Üí                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326‚Üí                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327‚Üí                } else {
328‚Üí                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329‚Üí                }
330‚Üí            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331‚Üí                Timber.w(e)
332‚Üí                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333‚Üí                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334‚Üí                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335‚Üí            }
336‚Üí            return this
337‚Üí        }
338‚Üí    }

16‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.preferences
17‚Üí
18‚Üíimport androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
19‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
20‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
21‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
22‚Üíimport com.ichi2.preferences.ControlPreference
23‚Üí
24‚Üíclass ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
25‚Üí    override val preferenceResource: Int
26‚Üí        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
27‚Üí    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
28‚Üí        get() = "prefs.controls"
29‚Üí
30‚Üí    override fun initSubscreen() {
31‚Üí        val commandMappingCategory = requirePreference<PreferenceCategory>(R.string.controls_command_mapping_cat_key)
32‚Üí        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(commandMappingCategory)
33‚Üí    }
34‚Üí
35‚Üí    /** Attaches all possible [ControlPreference] elements to a given [PreferenceCategory] */
36‚Üí    fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(category: PreferenceCategory) {
37‚Üí        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
38‚Üí            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
39‚Üí                setTitle(command.resourceId)
40‚Üí                key = command.preferenceKey
41‚Üí                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
42‚Üí            }
43‚Üí            category.addPreference(preference)
44‚Üí        }
45‚Üí    }
46‚Üí}

 19‚Üíimport android.annotation.SuppressLint

 25‚Üíimport com.afollestad.materialdialogs.customview.customView

 29‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand

 43‚Üí/**
 44‚Üí * A preference which allows mapping of inputs to actions (example: keys -> commands)
 45‚Üí *
 46‚Üí * This is implemented as a List, the elements allow the user to either add, or
 47‚Üí * remove previously mapped keys
 48‚Üí *
 49‚Üí * Future:
 50‚Üí * * Allow mapping gestures here
 51‚Üí * * Allow maps other than the reviewer
 52‚Üí */
 53‚Üíclass ControlPreference : ListPreference {
 54‚Üí    @Suppress("unused")
 55‚Üí    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 56‚Üí
 57‚Üí    @Suppress("unused")
 58‚Üí    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 59‚Üí
 60‚Üí    @Suppress("unused")
 61‚Üí    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 62‚Üí
 63‚Üí    @Suppress("unused")
 64‚Üí    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 65‚Üí
 66‚Üí    fun refreshEntries() {
 67‚Üí        val entryTitles: MutableList<CharSequence> = ArrayList()
 68‚Üí        val entryIndices: MutableList<Int> = ArrayList()
 69‚Üí        // negative indices are "add"
 70‚Üí        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_key))
 71‚Üí        entryIndices.add(ADD_KEY_INDEX)
 72‚Üí        // Put "Add gesture" option if gestures are enabled
 73‚Üí        if (context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean(GestureProcessor.PREF_KEY, false)) {
 74‚Üí            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_gesture))
 75‚Üí            entryIndices.add(ADD_GESTURE_INDEX)
 76‚Üí        }
 77‚Üí        // 0 and above are "delete" actions for already mapped preferences
 78‚Üí        for ((i, binding) in MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value).withIndex()) {
 79‚Üí            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_remove_binding, binding.toDisplayString(context)))
 80‚Üí            entryIndices.add(i)
 81‚Üí        }
 82‚Üí        entries = entryTitles.toTypedArray()
 83‚Üí        entryValues = entryIndices.map { it.toString() }.toTypedArray()
 84‚Üí    }
 85‚Üí
 86‚Üí    override fun onClick() {
 87‚Üí        refreshEntries()
 88‚Üí        super.onClick()
 89‚Üí    }
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí    /** The summary that appears on the preference */
 92‚Üí    override fun getSummary(): CharSequence = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
 93‚Üí        .joinToString(", ") { it.toDisplayString(context) }
 94‚Üí
 95‚Üí    /** Called when an element is selected in the ListView */
 96‚Üí    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
 97‚Üí    override fun callChangeListener(newValue: Any?): Boolean {
 98‚Üí        when (val index: Int = (newValue as String).toInt()) {
 99‚Üí            ADD_GESTURE_INDEX -> {
100‚Üí                val actionName = title
101‚Üí                MaterialDialog(context).show {
102‚Üí                    title(text = actionName.toString())
103‚Üí
104‚Üí                    val gesturePicker = GestureSelectionDialogUtils.getGesturePicker(context)
105‚Üí
106‚Üí                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { materialDialog ->
107‚Üí                        val gesture = gesturePicker.getGesture() ?: return@positiveButton
108‚Üí                        val mappableBinding = fromGesture(gesture)
109‚Üí                        if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
110‚Üí                            showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_gesture), materialDialog)
111‚Üí                        } else {
112‚Üí                            addBinding(mappableBinding)
113‚Üí                            dismiss()
114‚Üí                        }
115‚Üí                    }
116‚Üí                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
117‚Üí                    customView(view = gesturePicker)
118‚Üí
119‚Üí                    gesturePicker.onGestureChanged { gesture ->
120‚Üí                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(fromGesture(gesture))
121‚Üí                    }
122‚Üí
123‚Üí                    noAutoDismiss()
124‚Üí                }
125‚Üí            }
126‚Üí            ADD_KEY_INDEX -> {
127‚Üí                val actionName = title
128‚Üí                MaterialDialog(context).show {
129‚Üí                    val keyPicker: KeyPicker = KeyPicker.inflate(context)
130‚Üí                    customView(view = keyPicker.rootLayout)
131‚Üí                    title(text = actionName.toString())
132‚Üí
133‚Üí                    // When the user presses a key
134‚Üí                    keyPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
135‚Üí                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(CardSide.BOTH)))
136‚Üí                    }
137‚Üí
138‚Üí                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
139‚Üí                        val binding = keyPicker.getBinding() ?: return@positiveButton
140‚Üí                        // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
141‚Üí                        CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
142‚Üí                            val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
143‚Üí                            if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
144‚Üí                                showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), it)
145‚Üí                            } else {
146‚Üí                                addBinding(mappableBinding)
147‚Üí                                dismiss()
148‚Üí                            }
149‚Üí                        }
150‚Üí                    }
151‚Üí                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
152‚Üí
153‚Üí                    keyPicker.setKeycodeValidation(KeySelectionDialogUtils.disallowModifierKeyCodes())
154‚Üí                    noAutoDismiss()
155‚Üí                }
156‚Üí            }
157‚Üí            else -> {
158‚Üí                val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
159‚Üí                bindings.removeAt(index)
160‚Üí                value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
161‚Üí            }
162‚Üí        }
163‚Üí        // don't persist the value
164‚Üí        return false
165‚Üí    }
166‚Üí
167‚Üí    /**
168‚Üí     * Return if another command uses
169‚Üí     */
170‚Üí    private fun bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(binding: MappableBinding): Boolean {
171‚Üí        return getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding) != null
172‚Üí    }
173‚Üí
174‚Üí    /** Displays a warning to the user if the provided binding couldn't be used */
175‚Üí    private fun showToastIfBindingIsUsed(binding: MappableBinding) {
176‚Üí        val bindingCommand = getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)
177‚Üí            ?: return
178‚Üí
179‚Üí        val commandName = context.getString(bindingCommand.resourceId)
180‚Üí        val text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName)
181‚Üí        UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, text, true)
182‚Üí    }
183‚Üí
184‚Üí    /** @return command where the binding is mapped excluding the current command */
185‚Üí    private fun getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding: MappableBinding): ViewerCommand? {
186‚Üí        return MappableBinding.allMappings(context.sharedPrefs())
187‚Üí            // filter to the commands which have a binding matching this one except this
188‚Üí            .firstOrNull { x -> x.second.any { cmdBinding -> cmdBinding == binding } && x.first.preferenceKey != key }?.first
189‚Üí    }
190‚Üí
191‚Üí    private fun addBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
192‚Üí        val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
193‚Üí        // by removing the binding, we ensure it's now at the start of the list
194‚Üí        bindings.remove(binding)
195‚Üí        bindings.add(0, binding)
196‚Üí        value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
197‚Üí    }
198‚Üí
199‚Üí    /**
200‚Üí     * Remove binding from all control preferences
201‚Üí     * in the parent PreferenceGroup of this [ControlPreference]
202‚Üí     */
203‚Üí    private fun clearBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
204‚Üí        val preferenceGroup = parent
205‚Üí            ?: return
206‚Üí
207‚Üí        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
208‚Üí            val commandPreference = preferenceGroup.findPreference<ControlPreference>(command.preferenceKey)!!
209‚Üí            val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(commandPreference.value)
210‚Üí            if (binding in bindings) {
211‚Üí                bindings.remove(binding)
212‚Üí                commandPreference.value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
213‚Üí            }
214‚Üí        }
215‚Üí    }
216‚Üí
217‚Üí    private fun showDialogToReplaceBinding(binding: MappableBinding, title: String, parentDialog: MaterialDialog) {
218‚Üí        val commandName = context.getString(getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)!!.resourceId)
219‚Üí
220‚Üí        AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
221‚Üí            title(text = title)
222‚Üí            message(text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName))
223‚Üí            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_positive_replace) {
224‚Üí                clearBinding(binding)
225‚Üí                addBinding(binding)
226‚Üí                parentDialog.dismiss()
227‚Üí            }
228‚Üí            negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel)
229‚Üí        }
230‚Üí    }
231‚Üí
232‚Üí    companion object {
233‚Üí        private const val ADD_KEY_INDEX = -2
234‚Üí        private const val ADD_GESTURE_INDEX = -1
235‚Üí    }
236‚Üí}

 17‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18‚Üí
 19‚Üíimport android.content.SharedPreferences
 20‚Üíimport androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 21‚Üíimport androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 22‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
 23‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer
 24‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 25‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.AnswerButtons.*
 26‚Üíimport com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 27‚Üíimport com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 28‚Üíimport com.ichi2.utils.HandlerUtils
 29‚Üíimport timber.log.Timber
 30‚Üí
 31‚Üí/**
 32‚Üí * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 33‚Üí * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 34‚Üí *
 35‚Üí * ## Settings
 36‚Üí * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 37‚Üí *
 38‚Üí * ## Implementation
 39‚Üí *
 40‚Üí * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 41‚Üí * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 42‚Üí * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 43‚Üí *
 44‚Üí * When a card is displayed (Front)
 45‚Üí *
 46‚Üí * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 47‚Üí * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 48‚Üí *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 49‚Üí * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 50‚Üí *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 51‚Üí *
 52‚Üí * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 53‚Üí *
 54‚Üí * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 55‚Üí *
 56‚Üí * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 57‚Üí * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 58‚Üí *
 59‚Üí * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 60‚Üí * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 61‚Üí */
 62‚Üíclass AutomaticAnswer(
 63‚Üí    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 64‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 65‚Üí) {
 66‚Üí
 67‚Üí    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 68‚Üí     *
 69‚Üí     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 70‚Üí     */
 71‚Üí    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 72‚Üí        private set
 73‚Üí    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 74‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 75‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 76‚Üí            return@Runnable
 77‚Üí        }
 78‚Üí        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 79‚Üí    }
 80‚Üí    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 81‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 82‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 83‚Üí            return@Runnable
 84‚Üí        }
 85‚Üí        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
 86‚Üí    }
 87‚Üí
 88‚Üí    /**
 89‚Üí     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
 90‚Üí     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
 91‚Üí     */
 92‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
 93‚Üí    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
 94‚Üí
 95‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
 96‚Üí    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
 97‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 98‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 99‚Üí            return
100‚Üí        }
101‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
102‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
103‚Üí    }
104‚Üí
105‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
106‚Üí    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
107‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
108‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
109‚Üí            return
110‚Üí        }
111‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
112‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
113‚Üí    }
114‚Üí
115‚Üí    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
116‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
117‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
118‚Üí    }
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
121‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
122‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
123‚Üí    }
124‚Üí
125‚Üí    fun enable() {
126‚Üí        isDisabled = false
127‚Üí    }
128‚Üí
129‚Üí    fun disable() {
130‚Üí        isDisabled = true
131‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
132‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
133‚Üí    }
134‚Üí
135‚Üí    /** Stop any "Automatic show answer" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
136‚Üí    fun onDisplayQuestion() {
137‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
138‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceAnswer) return
139‚Üí
140‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
141‚Üí    }
142‚Üí
143‚Üí    /** Stop any "Automatic show question" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
144‚Üí    fun onDisplayAnswer() {
145‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
146‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceQuestion) return
147‚Üí
148‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
149‚Üí    }
150‚Üí
151‚Üí    // region TODO: These attempt to stop a race condition between a manual answer and the automated answer
152‚Üí    // I don't believe this is thread-safe
153‚Üí
154‚Üí    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has selected an answer */
155‚Üí    fun onSelectEase() {
156‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
157‚Üí    }
158‚Üí
159‚Üí    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has flipped the card */
160‚Üí    fun onShowAnswer() {
161‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
162‚Üí    }
163‚Üí
164‚Üí    // endregion
165‚Üí
166‚Üí    /**
167‚Üí     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowAnswer]
168‚Üí     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
169‚Üí     */
170‚Üí    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer(additionalDelay: Long = 0) {
171‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
172‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceAnswer) return
173‚Üí        delayedShowAnswer(settings.answerDelayMilliseconds + additionalDelay)
174‚Üí    }
175‚Üí
176‚Üí    /**
177‚Üí     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowQuestion]
178‚Üí     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
179‚Üí     */
180‚Üí    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion(additionalMediaDelay: Long = 0) {
181‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
182‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceQuestion) return
183‚Üí        delayedShowQuestion(settings.questionDelayMilliseconds + additionalMediaDelay)
184‚Üí    }
185‚Üí
186‚Üí    fun isEnabled(): Boolean {
187‚Üí        return settings.useTimer
188‚Üí    }
189‚Üí
190‚Üí    interface AutomaticallyAnswered {
191‚Üí        fun automaticShowAnswer()
192‚Üí        fun automaticShowQuestion(action: AutomaticAnswerAction)
193‚Üí    }
194‚Üí
195‚Üí    companion object {
196‚Üí        @CheckResult
197‚Üí        fun defaultInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered): AutomaticAnswer {
198‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswer(target, AutomaticAnswerSettings())
199‚Üí        }
200‚Üí

 17‚Üí

 30‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
